page: Microsoft Azure E8pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine belonging to the EPDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 307 GB disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across multi-core and memory-bound operations, contrasted by weaker single-core capabilities. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core score of 14512.41 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5993.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 566.05 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 91.15 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Conversely, single-core performance is weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1081.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 6226.55. Cryptographic and LLM inference tasks also perform weakly, with text generation on a 7B model reaching only 7.51 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E8pds_v5 leverages energy-efficient ARM architecture to deliver a high memory-to-core ratio, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require x86 instruction sets or high single-thread compute. The hardware profile is suited for database operations, as demonstrated by an average Passmark database score of 5718.7 and 10.98 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure E8pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the EPDSv5 family, featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio, alongside 307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support. Under benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14512.41 and a Redis SET throughput of 10.98 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is weak, as shown by its Geekbench single-core score of 1081.0. This memory-optimized profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive ARM-compatible workloads, such as database operations, caching layers, and static web serving, rather than compute-heavy or LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, built on Ampere Neoverse-N1.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for memory-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 CPU with 8 dedicated physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
Weak single-core and LLM inference benchmark results
Suited for database operations, caching, and static web serving
